risk_domain.name: Cross-Chain Identity Linkage via Bridge Usage
risk_domain.description: Detects privacy-breaking patterns in L1-to-L2 bridge usage that allow an observer to link addresses across chains, defeating the isolation that separate chains should provide

capabilities: full on-chain observation across L1 and multiple L2s
timing analysis on bridge deposits and withdrawals
amount correlation across chains with fee awareness
address clustering on each chain independently
limitations: no access to sequencer mempool (if private)

H1_same_address_bridge.name: same address bridge
H1_same_address_bridge.description: Identical addresses used across chains for bridging transactions.

signals.name: same_address_used
signals.description: Identical addresses used for deposits and withdrawals across different blockchain chains.

signals.name: ens_name_match
signals.description: L1 and L2 addresses resolve to the same ENS name or are part of the same ownership graph.

signals.name: deterministic_contract_match
signals.description: Smart contracts deployed at identical addresses on both chains due to deterministic deployment methods like CREATE2.

recommendations.description: Block or monitor suspicious addresses identified by the heuristic.
Deploy an address blacklist for known malicious addresses.
Enable monitoring of ENS names to detect potential bridge activities.
Implement address validation before allowing transactions across chains.
Ensure unique contract addresses on each chain to avoid deterministic match issues.

H2_amount_correlation.name: amount correlation
H2_amount_correlation.description: Specific or unique amounts transferred are indicative of a non-anonymous transaction.

signals.name: withdrawal_deposit_match
signals.description: Identifies if the L2 withdrawal amount matches the corresponding L1 deposit minus bridge fee within a specified time window.

signals.name: unique_amount_check
signals.description: Detects if the transaction amount has more than four significant decimal places, indicating it may be unique and non-anonymous.

signals.name: infrequent_amount_match
signals.description: Checks for bridged amounts that do not match typical transaction patterns in the deposit pool, suggesting potential non-anonymity.

signals.name: split_transaction_check
signals.description: Identifies if a large bridge transfer is split into multiple transactions with round numbers over different time windows, signaling non-anonymity.

recommendations.description: Round transaction amounts to standard denominations.
Ensure transactions use common pool amounts.
Combine multiple small transfers into one large transaction.
Use a tool to automatically round amounts before sending.
Always use the same deposit source for consistent amounts.

H3_bridge_sequence_fingerprint.name: bridge sequence fingerprint
H3_bridge_sequence_fingerprint.description: Repetitive bridge patterns across multiple chains suggest the same user or entity is involved.

signals.name: bridge_sequence
signals.description: Identify if an address or cluster repeatedly bridges to the same ordered set of Layer 2 (L2) chains.

signals.name: periodic_bridging
signals.description: Detect if bridge events occur at regular intervals, matching known user behavior patterns.

signals.name: multiple_L2s_short_window
signals.description: Find instances where the same address or cluster appears on three or more L2 chains within a short time frame.

signals.name: consistent_order_varying_timing
signals.description: Look for instances where the order of bridging to L2 chains is consistent, but the timing varies.

recommendations.description: Block the identified address to prevent further bridging activities.
Manually monitor the activity of the identified address for suspicious behavior.
Use security tokens to track and verify identities across blockchain networks.
Implement two-factor authentication for access to wallet services and transactions.

H4_gas_funding_post_bridge.name: gas funding post-bridge
H4_gas_funding_post_bridge.description: Funding of a newly bridged address from known bridge withdrawal addresses indicates non-anonymity.

signals.name: first_transfer
signals.description: An L2 address's first transaction is an ETH transfer.

signals.name: intermediary_funding
signals.description: Funding of an L2 address through a single intermediary that was previously funded by a bridge withdrawal.

signals.name: timed_funding
signals.description: Funding of an L2 address within minutes after a bridge withdrawal completes.

recommendations.description: Manually monitor newly bridged addresses for suspicious transactions.
Use a tool to automatically flag and analyze newly bridged addresses.
Implement a policy requiring a delay between bridge withdrawal and L2 funding.

H5_nft/token_bridge_linkage.name: NFT/token bridge linkage
H5_nft/token_bridge_linkage.description: Specific token transfers create identifiable links between chains, reducing anonymity.

signals.name: token_id_bridging
signals.description: Identifies unique token IDs across different blockchain chains.

signals.name: low_holder_erc20_bridge
signals.description: Detects ERC-20 token transfers from tokens with a small number of holders, indicating potential linking.

signals.name: specific_amount_transfer
signals.description: Picks up on specific token amounts that may be used as identifying markers.

recommendations.description: Automatically monitor for token bridge linkages.
Manually review suspicious transaction patterns.
Implement token blinding techniques to obscure IDs during transfers.
Distribute tokens widely among holders before bridging.
Utilize services designed to enhance privacy during cross-chain transfers.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring cross-chain linkability with all heuristics applied

methodology.step_1: Collect bridge transaction dataset across Arbitrum, Optimism, zkSync canonical bridges (N >= 10000 pairs)
methodology.step_2: Label ground truth linkages using known same-address bridges as positive examples
methodology.step_3: Run all 5 heuristics against each bridge event
methodology.step_5: Apply profile recommendations to a subset and re-measure
methodology.step_6: Compare: linkability rate before vs after countermeasures

combined_benchmark.target: reduce cross-chain linkability from estimated 80%+ to <15% with all recommendations applied

adversary_levels.name: basic
adversary_levels.description: Address reuse + unique token linkage only

templates.risk_assessment_block: BLOCKED: Bridge transaction creates {heuristic_name} linkage with {confidence}% confidence. {signal_description}. This bridge would link your L1 and L2 identities.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_bridges} bridge transactions. {n_linkable} create cross-chain linkages ({pct_linkable}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_bridges} bridge transactions pass privacy checks. No cross-chain linkage detected.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This reduces linkability by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
